what_it_does: Declares the RESOLUTION/detail axis as a module-level AXIS dict: id/namespace 'resolution', a single discrete field 'grain' (coarse|medium|fine), value_source 'live', and a long description defining grain as multi-resolution levels-of-detail with two facets (extraction-time forward-stepping and read-time multi-scale rollup) unified into one axis.
observations: AXIS dict: id='resolution', namespace='resolution', fields={'grain':'discrete'}, value_source='live'
grain values: coarse | medium | fine — described as 'the same thing zoomed', the MRL grain
desc records a dated decision (resolved 2026-06-22, 'Tim's STEPPED cascade')

Facet (a): EXTRACTION-TIME forward-stepping — coarse on all, step deeper in-pass per chunk by its own coarse output's kind (decision/spec/discussion->fine; log->stop), FORWARD-ONLY, never re-extract, no up-projection
Facet (b): READ-TIME multi-scale ROLLUP — a subset projection of the stored superset (determine-many)
Asserts the dragnet grain axis (recollection commit 4207e75) + multi-scale embeddings = ONE axis, not two; never-re-extract holds throughout
standouts: Unifies two facets (extraction forward-stepping and read-time rollup) into a single axis, with an explicit dated design decision and a referenced commit (4207e75)
Encodes the 'never re-extract / forward-only' invariant directly in the axis description
Per-chunk content-kind routing (decision/spec/discussion->fine, log->stop) embedded as the stepping rule
conventions: Follows the same AXIS declaration pattern as axes/register.py
Pure declarative data module, no logic
Carries design rationale and a provenance date/commit inside the desc string
concerns: Substantial design decision and invariants live only in a free-text desc string, making them non-machine-enforced from this file
notes: If briefing someone: this is the detail/grain axis — one axis covering both extraction-time forward-stepping (coarse->fine per chunk, never re-extract) and read-time multi-scale rollup.
questions: Where are the extraction-time stepping rules and read-time rollup actually implemented and enforced, since this file only declares the axis?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — module comment and AXIS dict describe the resolution/grain axis; the desc additionally records the unifying design decision.
intention_signals: Tries to collapse what could be two separate mechanisms (stepped extraction grain and multi-scale read rollup) into one declared axis with a forward-only never-re-extract guarantee.
novelty: One-axis-two-facets framing of resolution, plus a content-kind-driven in-pass forward-stepping extraction rule, recorded with its decision date and commit.
contribution: Contributes the resolution/grain axis declaration and the unified stepped-cascade detail model (extraction forward-stepping + read-time rollup, never re-extract).
summary_for_embedding: Python AXIS declaration for the resolution/grain axis: discrete coarse/medium/fine field, live value_source, unifying extraction-time forward-stepping and read-time multi-scale rollup into one never-re-extract levels-of-detail axis.
intention_for_embedding: To define detail/grain as a single live axis covering both stepped extraction and multi-scale read rollup under a forward-only never-re-extract invariant.
